mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals rapid labor market normalization, which accelerates consumer spending expectations and drives short-term S&P 500 upside.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls > 0.3pp in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite the unemployment drop, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 55% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and rising consumer credit balances indicate synchronized expansion in durable goods demand and household balance sheet health, which jointly lift equity multiples and drive positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the industrial production index above 1.5% confirms supply-side capacity utilization recovery, which expands corporate operating margins and sustains positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO growth exceeds the threshold, or the signal's information ratio falls below 0.2 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly validates upward earnings revisions, which serve as the primary fundamental driver of positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: The correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day SPX returns drops below 0.15 over any rolling 5-year window, or forward returns are negative in > 40% of positive GDP growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPIAUCSL exceeding 0.4% triggers immediate repricing of inflation expectations, forcing higher discount rates and compressing equity valuations, which produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of instances following the CPI threshold breach, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for VIX movements.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor demand strength, which reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive alpha in a factor-augmented model, or the interaction coefficient flips sign and becomes statistically insignificant over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand and reduced household wealth effects, which triggers earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 50% of triggered instances, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% when tested against a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 10-year period.
